# Figure description: Gamma V.jpg

**Source**: `36572_Smolianova_1984/geophysical sections/Gamma V.jpg`  
**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type

This is a **geophysical cross-section (профиль / profile)**, specifically a complex geological-geophysical interpreted profile combining multiple geophysical datasets. It appears to show gravity and/or magnetic anomaly curves plotted above a geological interpretation panel, with a stratigraphic/lithological table at the bottom. This is characteristic of Soviet-era regional geophysical survey profiles used to interpret subsurface structure.

---

## Coverage and Subject

The figure is labeled **"Профиль V"** (Profile V / Profile 5), indicating this is the fifth in a series of regional cross-sections across the survey area. The profile extends horizontally across the full width of the figure, with distance markers visible along the horizontal axis (appearing to extend to approximately **10,000+ meters** or ~10 km). The vertical axes show both elevation/depth values and geophysical anomaly amplitudes. The bottom panel contains a stratigraphic/geological column with formation designations (partially legible Russian text including what appear to be **ПРф** (possibly Precambrian/Proterozoic formations) and other abbreviated stratigraphic codes). The stamp in the upper right corner references **Report No. 36572**, confirming this belongs to the Smolianova et al. survey.

---

## Key Content

The figure contains **three primary data/interpretation panels stacked vertically**:

1. **Upper panel (geophysical anomaly curves):** Two curves are plotted above and through a zero baseline. The upper curve shows a broadly undulating anomaly profile with a notable **positive peak in the left-center portion** (labeled **"а1"** or similar annotation), rising approximately 4–6 units above baseline, then declining and rising again toward the right. A second, smoother curve runs slightly below it. These likely represent **Bouguer gravity anomaly** (Δg) or **magnetic field anomaly** (ΔT/Za) values, with the left vertical axis labeled in what appears to be **мГл (milligals)** or **нТл (nanotesla)** units, with scale markings at intervals of approximately 1–2 units. A sharp **rise toward the far right** of the profile is prominent, suggesting a significant structural or lithological boundary.

2. **Middle panel (interpreted geological section):** This panel shows a **subsurface geological interpretation** with two curves/boundaries: one shallower undulating line and one deeper, more irregular curve, defining interpreted geological layers or density/velocity interfaces. The curves diverge significantly toward the right side of the profile, suggesting **deepening of a basement surface** or thickening of a sedimentary sequence. Depth values on the left axis appear to range from approximately **0 to -1,000 m or deeper**, with negative values (below surface) extending downward. Several labeled points along the curves (numbered or lettered annotations such as **"2," "3," "4"** etc.) correspond to borehole intersections or control points.

3. **Lower panel (stratigraphic/lithological table):** A tabular section at the base of the figure contains columns with abbreviated formation names, velocity or density values (in units such as **г/см³** — grams per cubic centimeter, i.e., density), and stratigraphic age designations. Visible entries include:
   - Density values: **0.09 г/см³**, **0.006 г/см³**, **0.008 г/см³** (likely density contrast values)
   - Abbreviations: **ПРф**, **PR**, **PR_ф**, and similar — indicating **Proterozoic** formations
   - Depth intervals with values such as **-0.003(м)**, **10.009(м)** — possibly velocity or density parameters
   - Multiple columns separated by vertical lines, each corresponding to a segment of the profile

The overall pattern suggests the profile crosses a region with a **Proterozoic crystalline basement** overlain by younger sedimentary cover, with the basement deepening significantly to the east/right of the profile, possibly into a sedimentary basin.

**Title:** Профиль V (Profile V / Profile 5)

**Top left corner:** 1984 г. (year 1984)
